pattern: Two linear sweeps, one left-to-right and one right-to-left, propagate the distance from the nearest target seen so far, and the per-index minimum of the two passes is the answer.
hints: The closest target for an index is either somewhere to its left or somewhere to its right.
Scanning left to right you can record the distance to the most recent target seen; scanning right to left does the same for targets ahead.
Do a forward pass keeping the index of the last target (distance grows by one each step) and a backward pass keeping the next target's index, then take the smaller distance at every position.

edge_inputs.description: target appears multiple times with a tie in the middle
single character that is the target
target at the very start so all distances grow rightward
every character is the target so all distances are zero
two separated targets create a peak between them

stress.description: random strings guaranteed to contain the target character
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    c = rng.choice('abcde')
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(n))
    if c not in s:
        pos = rng.randrange(n)
        s = s[:pos] + c + s[pos + 1:]
    return [s, c]
stress.note: ensures the guarantee that c occurs at least once holds
